meta_title: Impact Doors in Cresthaven, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Cresthaven, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote today!
h1: Cresthaven's Trusted Source for Hurricane-Grade Impact Doors That Last
intro:
Cresthaven may sit roughly four miles from the Atlantic shoreline, but that distance offers little comfort when a major hurricane sweeps through Broward County. With 10 named storms impacting this area since 1990 — including the catastrophic one-two punch of Hurricane Andrew in 1992 and Hurricane Wilma in 2005 — residents here know all too well what happens when homes aren't prepared. Impact doors are no longer a luxury upgrade for Cresthaven homeowners; they are a fundamental layer of protection for the people and property inside. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' brings that protection directly to your doorstep.

The Cresthaven community is made up predominantly of single-family homes, many of which were built before the sweeping code changes that followed Hurricane Andrew. In fact, an estimated 55% of homes in this ZIP code (33060) were constructed prior to 1994 — a year that fundamentally changed how Florida builds for wind resistance. Those pre-code homes often feature hollow-core entry doors, aging sliding glass doors, and French doors that simply weren't engineered to withstand the 170 mph wind loads that Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) designation demands. For a neighborhood where the average home is valued at approximately $290,000, the financial and personal stakes of leaving those vulnerabilities unaddressed are simply too high.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing specializes in the full range of impact-resistant door solutions — from reinforced entry doors and multi-point locking French doors to heavy-duty sliding glass doors — all engineered and tested to HVHZ standards. Our team has completed installations in more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we understand the specific demands that Cresthaven's climate, building codes, and neighborhood character place on every project. Whether you live steps from Cresthaven Park or on a quiet residential block near Pompano Beach's western edge, we have the product, the expertise, and the service commitment to get the job done right.

section_why.h2: Why Cresthaven Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact door contractor isn't just about picking a product off a shelf — it's about trusting a team to handle permits, precision installation, and long-term performance in one of the most demanding wind environments in the United States. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation in Broward County on exactly that kind of end-to-end accountability. Our installers are 100% in-house employees, never subcontractors, which means the same experienced hands that walk your property for the initial assessment are the ones doing the work on installation day. With over 60 years of combined experience across our installation team, that continuity makes a measurable difference in quality and attention to detail.

Many Cresthaven homeowners are navigating the door replacement process for the first time and aren't sure where to begin. Our consultants will walk you through every step — from selecting the right door style and glass package for your home's layout to submitting the necessary permits through the Broward County Building Division. We handle the paperwork, coordinate inspections, and ensure every installation meets or exceeds the Florida Building Code's strict HVHZ requirements. You never have to wonder whether your new door will pass inspection, because we've designed our entire process around getting it right the first time.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and has completed impact door installations across communities throughout Broward County, including neighboring Pompano Beach, Coconut Creek, and Deerfield Beach. That regional experience means we understand how local inspectors evaluate HVHZ installations, what common pitfalls look like in older homes like many in Cresthaven, and how to address framing, threshold, and rough opening challenges that frequently arise in pre-1994 construction. When you work with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're working with a team that's seen it all — and has the track record to prove it.

Our commitment to service doesn't end when the last screw is set. We stand behind every installation with manufacturer warranties on products from ESW and CWI, supplemented by our own workmanship guarantee. From your first call to the final walk-through, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers the kind of professional experience that earns referrals throughout neighborhoods like Cresthaven — because in a tight-knit community, your neighbors will ask who did your doors.

section_history.h2: Cresthaven's Storm History Makes a Strong Case for Impact Door Upgrades
section_history.body:
The storm history that has shaped Cresthaven and the broader Broward County region reads like a reminder of how real and recurring the threat is. Hurricane Andrew made landfall in 1992 as one of the most destructive storms ever to strike the United States, and while its epicenter hit Miami-Dade County hardest, the destruction it caused throughout South Florida revealed a systemic failure in residential construction standards. Thousands of doors — entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — failed catastrophically, not from direct hits but from pressure differentials and wind-driven debris that these products were never designed to handle. Andrew's legacy is the modern Florida Building Code and the HVHZ designation that Cresthaven falls under today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought a different kind of challenge to Broward County communities. Wilma moved rapidly across the state and produced sustained winds exceeding 120 mph across much of South Florida, causing widespread power outages, roof damage, and door and window failures in homes that had still not been upgraded since Andrew. Then Hurricane Irma in 2017 once again tested the resilience of older Broward County housing stock, with many pre-code homes experiencing flooding through failed door seals and structural stress that compromised frames and thresholds. For Cresthaven residents in homes built before 1994 — which accounts for the majority of housing in the area — these storms served as expensive, often traumatic lessons in the cost of inadequate door protection.

With 10 storms impacting this region since 1990, the question for Cresthaven homeowners is no longer whether another major hurricane will come, but whether their home will be ready when it does. Impact doors represent the single most effective upgrade available for older entry points, combining laminated impact-resistant glass, reinforced frames, and multi-point locking hardware to create a system that holds under conditions that would destroy standard residential doors. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has helped Cresthaven families close the gap left by decades of inadequate construction standards, one door at a time.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Cresthaven and Broward County
section_code.body:
Cresthaven's inclusion in Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone is not a bureaucratic label — it carries real, enforceable requirements for every door replacement or new installation performed in the area. The HVHZ designation applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties and mandates compliance with the most stringent wind and impact resistance standards in the state. Under the Florida Building Code, all exterior doors installed in HVHZ-designated areas must carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) approval or a Florida Product Approval (FL Number), demonstrating that they have passed rigorous large missile impact testing, cyclic wind pressure testing, and water infiltration standards. Standard doors — even those marketed as 'storm doors' — do not meet this threshold.

For Cresthaven specifically, doors must be rated to withstand a design wind speed of 170 mph, consistent with the county's wind zone classification. This means both the door product itself and the installation method must comply with the manufacturer's approved installation drawings, which are reviewed as part of the permitting process through the Broward County Building Division. Permits are required for all exterior door replacements in Broward County, and inspections are mandatory before the project can be considered complete. Homeowners who skip the permit process risk complications when selling their home or filing insurance claims after storm damage — making proper permitting not just a legal obligation but a practical financial safeguard.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit and inspection process on behalf of Cresthaven homeowners, ensuring that every installation is fully documented, code-compliant, and inspection-ready. Our installers are trained specifically in HVHZ installation protocols and work exclusively with products that carry the necessary approvals. When the Broward County inspector signs off on your new impact doors, you can have full confidence that your home meets the highest wind protection standard in Florida — not just on paper, but in practice.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Brands Built for Cresthaven's HVHZ Demands
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of the most respected names in the South Florida market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for the extreme wind and impact requirements of Florida's HVHZ, and both carry the Miami-Dade NOA approvals and Florida Product Approval certifications required for legal installation in Cresthaven. These are not off-the-shelf products adapted for hurricane country — they are purpose-built systems designed from the ground up to perform under the conditions that Broward County homeowners face every storm season.

Our impact entry doors from ESW and CWI feature heavy-gauge aluminum or fiberglass frames with thermally broken construction to reduce heat transfer, making them as energy-efficient as they are storm-resistant. The laminated impact glass used in vision panels and sidelights is constructed with a polyvinyl butyral (PVB) or ionoplast interlayer that holds the glass together on impact, preventing penetration and maintaining the door's structural integrity even when the outer lite is cracked. Multi-point locking systems — engaging at the top, middle, and bottom of the door frame simultaneously — distribute wind load across the full frame rather than concentrating stress at a single latch point, dramatically reducing the risk of door failure during high-pressure storm events.

For Cresthaven homeowners looking to upgrade sliding glass doors or French doors, both ESW and CWI offer HVHZ-compliant options that combine impact resistance with smooth, low-maintenance operation. Sliding doors feature reinforced meeting rails, heavy-duty rollers, and interlocking sill systems that prevent water infiltration even under driving rain conditions. French doors are available in a range of panel configurations and glass options, allowing homeowners to balance aesthetics with performance. Whether you're replacing a single aging entry door on a modest Cresthaven bungalow or upgrading every exterior opening on a larger home, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can source and install the right ESW or CWI product for your specific situation.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Cresthaven Homeowners
section_cost.body:
The cost of impact door installation in Cresthaven varies depending on the type and number of doors being replaced, the complexity of the opening, and the specific product selected. For a standard impact entry door with sidelights, homeowners in this area can typically expect to invest between $1,800 and $4,500 fully installed, including permitting fees. Sliding glass door replacements generally range from $2,500 to $6,000 depending on door width and glass configuration, while French door systems run similarly. These figures reflect the HVHZ-grade products and professional installation required for code compliance in Broward County — and they represent a meaningful investment in a home valued at around $290,000.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options designed to make impact door upgrades accessible for Cresthaven's lower-middle income homeowners without requiring a large upfront payment. Financing starts as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available to qualified applicants, allowing you to spread the cost of your investment over time while your home is protected immediately. Our financing partners offer competitive rates and terms that can be tailored to fit a range of budgets, and our consultants will walk you through available options during your free in-home assessment — no pressure, no obligation.

Beyond the immediate protection benefit, impact door installations in Cresthaven generate meaningful financial returns over time. Homeowners consistently report reductions in homeowner's insurance premiums following impact door installation — in some cases saving hundreds of dollars per year on wind coverage alone. Energy savings from better door sealing and thermally efficient frames also reduce monthly utility costs. When combined with potential increases in resale value — buyers in Broward County increasingly expect impact-rated openings in the homes they purchase — the long-term return on a quality impact door installation is substantial. For most Cresthaven homeowners, the question isn't whether they can afford to upgrade; it's whether they can afford not to.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Cresthaven Impact Door Owners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible benefits of installing HVHZ-compliant impact doors in Cresthaven is the potential for significant homeowner's insurance savings. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer wind mitigation discounts to policyholders who have made qualifying improvements to their homes, and impact-rated doors are among the upgrades that directly affect your wind mitigation inspection score. A wind mitigation report, completed by a licensed inspector after your impact door installation, documents your home's opening protection and submits the findings to your insurance carrier for premium recalculation. For Cresthaven homeowners in Broward County — where wind coverage costs are among the highest in the state — these discounts can be substantial.

Common insurance carriers operating in the Broward County market, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, all recognize opening protection upgrades as a factor in wind mitigation credits. The specific discount amount varies by carrier and policy structure, but homeowners who upgrade from unprotected openings to fully impact-rated doors and windows frequently see annual savings ranging from $300 to over $1,000 depending on their coverage level and prior premium. For a Cresthaven homeowner paying elevated wind premiums on a pre-1994 home, those savings can meaningfully offset the cost of the installation over just a few years.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing recommends that every Cresthaven homeowner who completes an impact door installation schedule a wind mitigation inspection promptly upon project completion. We can provide the necessary product documentation — including Florida Product Approval numbers and Miami-Dade NOA references for your specific ESW or CWI doors — to support your inspector's report and ensure your carrier receives accurate information. Beyond the immediate premium discount, fully protected openings also reduce your out-of-pocket exposure in the event of a storm claim, since a door that holds during a hurricane is a door that doesn't generate a costly replacement or interior water damage claim. Impact doors are, in every sense, an insurance investment.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact doors in Cresthaven, or will standard storm doors meet code?
faqs.a: Cresthaven falls within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which legally requires all exterior doors to carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval certification. Standard storm doors — even those rated for moderate wind zones — do not meet this requirement. Any door replacement in Cresthaven must be a true impact-rated product that has passed the HVHZ large missile and cyclic pressure testing protocols. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing only installs products that meet these standards.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to replace impact doors in Cresthaven, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Cresthaven typically ranges from $1,800 to $6,000 per opening depending on door type, size, and product selection, with permits included. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants. Our consultants provide detailed, no-obligation quotes during a free in-home assessment, and we'll walk you through financing terms that fit your budget so you can move forward with confidence.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my front door or sliding glass door in Cresthaven?
faqs.a: Yes. All exterior door replacements in Broward County require a permit from the Broward County Building Division, including in Cresthaven. An inspection is also required after installation to confirm the work meets HVHZ standards. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application and inspection coordination process on your behalf, so you don't have to navigate the paperwork yourself. Skipping the permit process can create complications with insurance claims and home resale.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Cresthaven home?
faqs.a: Most impact door installations in Cresthaven are completed in a single day per opening, though larger projects involving multiple doors may take two days. The overall timeline from signed contract to completed installation typically runs three to six weeks, with most of that time spent in the permitting phase with Broward County. Once permits are approved, our in-house installation team schedules your project promptly. We keep you informed at every stage so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Cresthaven?
faqs.a: Very likely, yes. Installing HVHZ-compliant impact doors qualifies as an opening protection upgrade under Florida's wind mitigation credit program. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your improvements and submit a report to your insurance carrier, which is required to offer discounts for qualifying upgrades. Broward County homeowners in pre-1994 homes frequently see significant premium reductions — sometimes hundreds of dollars annually — after upgrading to impact-rated doors and windows.
